channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Traffic light positioned in the wrong place which halts traffic, 23rd June
item.description:
Hello, Just a suggestion which will help ease traffic flow, basically as you come from burges road to barking road, to the left there is a traffic light, now if pedestrians press it and it turns red, the cars that come out of burges road are stuck as only 2 cars can get out if even that to be honest as there is a constant traffic flow on barking road. This is frustrating as the cars on burges road are then stuck again waiting for cars to stop flowing on barking road. My suggestion is to place the traffic light before burges road, where the barking park is, now if the traffic light does turn red, the burges road cars can get out with ease which will help ease the traffic flow on burges road. Didnt really make sense to put a traffic light just after the road. Please consider this suggestion as it is perfect for all, if more explanation is needed or photos, I will gladly supply them. Thank You
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Barking Road, Little Ilford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish including garden rubbish dumped in parking area, 23rd June
item.description:
It is dangerous as it attracts rats and other wild animals. It is also a hazard. Just noticed it today.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Melbury Drive, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned rubbish at 12 Park Ave, 23rd June
item.description:
A pile of abandoned rubbish in front of house No 12 Park Ave N13 about 3-4 months ago and remains. Old carpet, a pink baby bath and a plastic dolls house ( scribbled on with pen). Looks unsightly, like a slum.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large panels of hardboard dumped on path, 23rd June
item.description:
Weekly fly tipping on Charlton Park Lane. Large panels of hardboard have been dumped on the pathway causing a hazard and are unsightly too.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Park Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped waste, 23rd June
item.description:
Waste

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bull Lane, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeatedly reported over 2 months - not “fixed”, 23rd June
item.description:
I have repeatedly reported this dumped corrugated material over the past few months and it has still not been collected. One of the updates even stated it was “fixed”. This was not true. Please arrange collection of these materials. It is unacceptable that it has still not been collected after such a long period of time.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Park Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture fly tipping, 23rd June
item.description:
Fly tipping of furniture needs to be collected asap. Persistent issues on this corner council need to monitor and introduce signage

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balchen Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on telecoms units, 23rd June
item.description:
Please remove. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Paul's Wood Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green Lane controversial yellow box should be reviewed and potentially changed due to the serious issues. Greenwich Council should review the junction layout with respect to data about usage, congestion, road safety and traffic impact. Greenwich Council planners need to recognise the real world effects of their designs, which can only be understood through a full post implementation review. There is a need to assess the wider impacts on traffic flow, accessibility and amend, redesign or remove elements of the scheme for significant safety or operational concerns identified. by the community to generally restore more efficient, safe and predictable traffic movement at the Green Lane junction with William Barefoot Drive., 23rd June
item.description:
Green Lane controversial yellow box should be reviewed and potentially changed due to the serious issues. Greenwich Council should review the junction layout with respect to data about usage, congestion, road safety and traffic impact. Greenwich Council planners need to recognise the real world effects of their designs, which can only be understood through a full post implementation review. There is a need to assess the wider impacts on traffic flow, accessibility and amend, redesign or remove elements of the scheme for significant safety or operational concerns identified. by the community to generally restore more efficient, safe and predictable traffic movement at the Green Lane junction with William Barefoot Drive.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Raised paving slab, 23rd June
item.description:
Slab raised with stones holding it up. Next to tha.es water access chamber. On pavement next to road rather than by houses. Please fix. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 127, St Paul's Wood Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Men sleeping in vehicles for 2 + years, please help!, 23rd June
item.description:
2+ vehicles have been parked along Beaconsfield Road constantly causing a noise and hygiene issue. These men are living in their cars, using the woods as a toilets, being noisy, taking up parking spaces for residents and intimidating families. Other cars are constantly selling drugs and smoking drugs whilst driving. One blue motor home and one black car. Council needs to house them and get them off the street- making it unsafe for families.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green Lane controversial yellow box should be reviewed and potentially changed due to the serious issues. Greenwich Council should review the junction layout with respect to data about usage, congestion, road safety and traffic impact. Greenwich Council planners need to recognise the real world effects of their designs, which can only be understood through a full post implementation review. There is a need to assess the wider impacts on traffic flow, accessibility and amend, redesign or remove elements of the scheme for significant safety or operational concerns identified. by the community to generally restore more efficient, safe and predictable traffic movement at the Green Lane junction with William Barefoot Drive., 23rd June
item.description:

Greenwich Council planners need to recognise the real world effects of their designs, which can only be understood through a full post implementation review. There is a need to assess the wider impacts on traffic flow, accessibility and amend, redesign or remove elements of the scheme for significant safety or operational concerns identified. by the community to generally restore more efficient, safe and predictable traffic movement at the Green Lane junction with William Barefoot Drive.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham

item.title: Dog foul on the pavement at Elmer's end road/Croydon road, 23rd June
item.description:
There is dog foul on the pavements that has been there for weeks. It is on both roads outside sherborne court and in the surrounding streets also. It is like playing very high risk hopskotch. There is never a day that there isn't dog foul and there is also a lot of litter that stays for weeks and never seems to be cleaned in the same corner. It is a serious safety hazard at this point especially for children who are closer to the ground.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No tax and no MOT, 23rd June
item.description:
Car has been parked outside my house for 3-4 months. The car has no tax and no MOT yet is sitting on the road. I reported this and was told that it was a dvla issue due to the driver being un-registered. I have reported to the DVLA multiple times and nothing has been done. This car should not be on the road. Please remove it! Blocking parking on a road that has limited parking.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chapman Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter everywhere constantly, 23rd June
item.description:
This entire street up to Sainsbury’s is perpetually covered in litter. It needs daily attention
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thistlebrook, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees need pruning, 23rd June
item.description:
Trees lining Elmstead woods leading towards the alley are overhanging Beaconsfield Road, they have not been pruned for 2 + years. Trees outside 315-321 Beaconsfield Road are also seriously overgrown and keep breaking in the storm. Dangerous for residents as branches will cause serious damage.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress left in common fly tipping area, 23rd June
item.description:
I've not had to report anything for a while, but unfortunately someone left a mattress on the pavement. I noticed it at 8am this morning and it wasn't there when I walked past at 8pm the night before.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tooley Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van reverse into pole sign. Pole is now tilted and dangerous, 23rd June
item.description:
Happened last week Pole looks unsafe It is on a tilt

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Belsize Lane, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bexleyheath Thames Water Fountain not working, 23rd June
item.description:
The Thames Water Fountain is not working and with the very hot weather not being use the fountain to get water is a danger to life. This water fountain is close to Barclays Bank.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-5, Market Place, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Entrance for parking, 23rd June
item.description:
The post are too narrow when u need to park the car While u entered the lanridge road It's on the kerb lefthand side Is it anyway you can widen the pole /post
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lanridge Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
